current.summary: Designing intelligent conflict detection for --to-all flag: need to verify which supported providers use ~/.agents/ across all content types before implementing path-conflict warnings and user choice logic

activeWork.description: Research provider ~/.agents/ usage patterns across all content types (skills, rules, mcp, commands, prompts, hooks, agents) to establish verified baseline for conflict detection logic in --to-all installation
activeWork.progress: Identified critical gap in current approach: assumed only Codex uses ~/.agents/ as primary path, but Gemini/Windsurf/Amp/RooCode all read from ~/.agents/skills/. Need to verify this pattern holds across all content types and all 5 supported providers (Codex, Amp, Gemini, Windsurf, RooCode) with official documentation before implementing conflict detection. Current syllago code verified to show skills-only cross-provider sharing; need to research whether ~/.agents/ convention extends to rules, mcp, commands, prompts, hooks, agents.
blockers: No official documentation verified for which providers use ~/.agents/ across content types beyond skills

recentDecisions: Avoid 'virtual provider' terminology; use standard cross-provider shared directory or generic agents directory naming
Conflict detection must apply to all content types, not just skills
Design should support three outcomes: (a) warn user upfront, (b) let user choose install path, (c) default intelligently based on what's installed
Must verify against official provider docs before implementing - don't assume current patterns
nextSteps: Research official Codex/Gemini/Windsurf/Amp/RooCode docs for ~/.agents/ usage across all content types
Verify Gemini/Windsurf/RooCode actually read ~/.agents/ for other content types or if it's skills-only convention
Map install paths vs discovery paths for each provider/content-type combination
Design conflict detection algorithm with verified baseline
Implement user-choice flow for conflicting install paths in --to-all
Update Provider struct with SharedReadPaths field if needed

context.notes: Resumed from benchmark-installer-implementation handoff. Background: --to-all flag is implemented and tested (all 4 layers complete, 17 skills installed to 7 providers). Current issue: Codex installs skills to ~/.agents/skills/, but Gemini/Windsurf/Amp/RooCode all read from that same path, causing conflicts. Need intelligent conflict detection that: (1) detects when multiple selected providers share install/read paths, (2) verifies this across all content types via official docs, (3) warns user and lets them choose or defaults intelligently based on what's installed on their system. Research blocker: need to confirm ~/.agents/ convention is real across content types and providers before coding solution.

previousSessions.summary: Benchmark installer implementation: all 4 layers complete, 17 skills imported to 7 providers, Gemini conflict discovered
completedWork: Layer 2: Fixed syllabo add --from <registry-name> bug (commit 0b8c449), imported and installed 17 benchmark skills
Layer 4: Created roadmap bead syllago-k55bj, updated README with benchmarking workflow
nextSteps: Design conflict detection for --to-all when providers share install paths
Research provider ~/.agents/ usage patterns across all content types
